about.hero_title: The New Era of Cosmetic Curation.
about.hero_desc: Careful Baza Labs is not just a brand. We are a global selection laboratory dedicated to finding the most advanced formulas in the world.
about.sec1_title: From the origin lab straight to your skin.
about.sec1_p1: The traditional beauty market is saturated with middlemen. Each step adds exorbitant profit margins that you pay for.
about.sec1_p2: At Careful Baza Labs, we redesigned consumer engineering. We test and approve only what delivers real clinical results.
about.pillar1_d: We seek excellence wherever it may be, from Asian innovation to European purity.
about.pillar2_d: We reject 90% of the products we test. We analyze the concentration of active ingredients and the absence of toxins.
about.pillar3_d: Clinical-grade active ingredients with unparalleled value in the global market.

skin.title: Ultimate Guide: Skin Types
skin.subtitle: Understand your face's biology for a high-performance routine.
skin.intro: Identifying your skin type is the first step to stop wasting money on the wrong products. The skin is a living, dynamic organ, and its needs change. Below, we've mapped the most common profiles and the exact science to treat them.

oily.title: Oily Skin and Enlarged Pores
oily.body: Characterized by the overproduction of sebum by the sebaceous glands. Excessive shine appears a few hours after washing, and pores become visible. The most common mistake is washing the face several times a day with strong soaps, which removes the natural protection and causes a rebound effect.
oily.tip: Avoid coarse scrubs. They create micro-lesions that inflame the pores. Prefer gentle chemical exfoliation and ultra-light gel hydration.
oily.why: Niacinamide penetrates the pores and tells the gland to reduce oil production, while Zinc works by drying out active breakouts.

dry.title: Dry Skin and Compromised Barrier
dry.body: Unlike dehydrated skin (which only needs water), dry skin doesn't produce enough lipids (natural oils). Without this natural "cement", the water you drink easily evaporates through the skin. It feels tight, has a rough texture, and flakes.
dry.tip: Never wash your face with hot water in the shower. High temperatures melt the few natural oils your skin has managed to produce to defend itself.
dry.why: Combines Hyaluronic Acid (to pull in water) and Ceramides (to lock that water inside), restoring comfort from the first use.

sensitive.title: Sensitive and Red Skin
sensitive.body: Sensitive skin is a condition of inflammation. The protective barrier is so thin that wind, cosmetics, and even stress easily penetrate, triggering alerts that cause redness and burning. The focus here is not to renew, but to soothe.
sensitive.tip: Adopt minimalism (Skin Diet). When the skin is in a burning crisis, suspend acids and use only a mild soap and a soothing moisturizer.
sensitive.why: Its botanical prebiotics feed the "good bacteria" of the skin, creating a protective layer that reduces redness instantly.

ingredients.title: Clinical Dictionary of Ingredients
ingredients.subtitle: Total transparency about what truly transforms your skin.
ingredients.intro: The cosmetic industry profits from empty promises and complicated names. At Careful Baza Labs, we translate the science so you know exactly what you are investing in.

niacinamide.title: Niacinamide (Vitamin B3)
niacinamide.body: It's like the "jack-of-all-trades" of skincare. It lightens dark spots, visually reduces pore size, and calms inflamed breakouts. The best part? It doesn't irritate and can be used in the sun.
niacinamide.tip: Niacinamide serums mix well with everything. You can combine it with Vitamin C in the morning or Retinol at night without fear of a reaction.
niacinamide.why: A clinical dose of 10% guarantees visible results in 14 days for pores and uneven texture, with zero burning.

retinol.title: Retinol (Vitamin A)
retinol.body: The only scientifically proven active ingredient that truly reverses aging. It enters the cell and tells it to "work faster", swapping old skin for new. It thins the top layer (giving glow) and thickens the bottom layer (stimulating collagen).
retinol.tip: If you are a beginner with Retinol, use the "Sandwich" technique: Apply moisturizer, then Retinol, and then moisturizer on top again. This prevents flaking.
retinol.why: We envelope the active ingredient in nanocapsules. It enters the skin and only opens deep down, guaranteeing new collagen without leaving the face red.

hair.title: The Science of Trichology
hair.subtitle: Diagnosing the structural health of your strands.
hair.intro: Knowing if your hair is straight or curly is just aesthetics. To truly treat it, we need to look under the microscope and understand porosity (the level of damage to the "coat" that protects the strand).

porous.title: Porous and Damaged Hair (With Frizz)
porous.body: Imagine a hair strand like a roof. In healthy hair, the tiles (cuticles) are tightly closed. In porous hair (due to flat irons, bleaching, or sun), the tiles are lifted. Shower water enters easily but evaporates in seconds, leaving the strand dry and puffy.
porous.tip: Do the cup test: place a loose strand of hair in a glass of water. If it sinks quickly, it's porous (needs oil and reconstruction). If it floats on top, it's low porosity (only needs light hydration).
porous.why: Dense vegetable oils act as a "glue" for the hair tiles, sealing the cuticles and preventing moisture from escaping throughout the day.

fine.title: Fine, Oily and Flat Hair
fine.body: Fine hair is very delicate and has less "body". Because the strand is very smooth and light, the natural oils produced by the scalp slide down to the ends very quickly, leaving the hair looking flat on the same day it was washed.
fine.tip: The golden rule for fine hair: Conditioner should never touch from the middle to the top of the head. Apply only to the extreme ends so as not to weigh down the roots.
fine.why: Being 100% free of heavy silicones, it detangles fine hair bringing softness, without adding the weight of traditional creams.

tips.title: Routine Protocols
tips.subtitle: The engineering behind flawless skincare.
tips.intro: Buying expensive products and applying them in the wrong order throws money in the trash. The universal rule of cosmetology is simple: always apply from the most liquid product to the thickest.

morning.title: Morning Routine (Attack & Defense)
morning.body: The goal in the morning is not to treat severe problems, it's to protect your face from the sun, car smoke, and cell phone light. The correct protocol is: 1. Gentle Cleansing > 2. Antioxidant (e.g., Vitamin C) > 3. Light Moisturizer > 4. Sunscreen.
morning.tip: If your skin is normal to dry, you don't need to use soap in the morning! Just wash your face with cold water. This saves the hydration your skin built while you slept.
morning.why: Niacinamide acts as a shield that protects cells against the darkening of spots caused by daily radiation and pollution.

night.title: Night Routine (Repair & Restore)
night.body: During deep sleep, the blood flow to your face increases and the skin enters "repair mode". This is the time to put strong active ingredients (like acids) to work. The order is: 1. Deep Cleansing > 2. Specific Treatment (Serums) > 3. Rich Moisturizing Cream.
night.tip: The Korean secret of Double Cleansing: Use an oil-based product first to melt makeup and sunscreen, and then wash with your gel facial cleanser to truly clean the pores.
night.why: Used as the last step at night, it creates an invisible "blanket" over the skin, preventing water and serums from evaporating while you sleep.
